questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a component of Human Development Index (HDI)?
questions.explanation: HDI includes life expectancy, education level, and per capita income, not export earnings.

questions.question: Why is per capita income considered an average?

options.A: It shows income of richest person
options.B: It divides total income by total population

questions.explanation: Per capita income is calculated by dividing total income by total population.

questions.question: Which indicator best reflects health conditions in a country?
questions.explanation: Life expectancy indicates overall health conditions.

questions.question: Which of the following shows unequal distribution of income?

options.C: Large gap between rich and poor

questions.explanation: A large gap between rich and poor shows income inequality.

questions.question: Infant Mortality Rate (IMR) measures:

options.A: Deaths of children below one year per 1000 live births

questions.explanation: IMR measures deaths of infants below one year per 1000 live births.

questions.question: Which statement is true about development goals?

options.A: They are same for all people
options.D: They are decided by government only

questions.explanation: Different people have different development goals.

questions.question: Which country is likely to be more developed?
questions.explanation: High HDI and literacy indicate higher development.

questions.question: Sustainable development aims to:

options.B: Meet present needs without harming future generations

questions.explanation: Sustainable development protects future generations.

questions.question: Which resource is considered non-renewable?
questions.explanation: Petroleum is a non-renewable resource.

questions.question: Public facilities are important because they:
questions.explanation: Public facilities improve overall living standards.

questions.question: Which of the following best shows education development?
questions.explanation: Literacy rate reflects education level.

questions.question: HDI compares countries based on:
questions.explanation: HDI uses income, health, and education indicators.

questions.question: Which of the following is a limitation of per capita income?
questions.explanation: It does not show income distribution.

questions.question: A country with high IMR indicates:
questions.explanation: High IMR shows poor health facilities.

questions.question: Which factor is both income and non-income related?
questions.explanation: Employment provides income and improves quality of life.

questions.question: Which country will have better development?
questions.explanation: High literacy and low IMR show better development.

questions.question: Which organization calculates HDI?
questions.explanation: UNDP calculates and publishes HDI.

questions.question: Which of the following improves sustainable development?
questions.explanation: Rainwater harvesting conserves natural resources.

questions.question: Higher per capita income does not guarantee:
questions.explanation: High income does not always ensure good health facilities.

questions.question: Which measure is more comprehensive than per capita income?
questions.explanation: HDI includes income, health, and education.

questions.question: What does literacy rate measure?
questions.explanation: Literacy rate measures education level.

questions.question: Which of the following is a renewable resource?
questions.explanation: Wind is a renewable resource.

questions.question: Equitable development means:
questions.explanation: Equitable development ensures equal opportunities.

questions.question: Which factor indicates economic inequality?
questions.explanation: A large income gap shows inequality.

questions.question: Development is measured using:
questions.explanation: Development uses multiple indicators like income, health, and education.
